message:
fix: add drift check to hive-deploy so stale installed scripts get synced (#168)

The deploy script only synced scripts when git pull brought new commits.
If files at /usr/local/bin/ were already stale before the timer was set
up, they'd never get fixed. Now every run also compares each installed
script to the repo copy and syncs any that differ.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -26,30 +26,35 @@ git pull --rebase origin main --quiet 2>/dev/null || {
 }
 AFTER=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
 
-if [ "$BEFORE" = "$AFTER" ]; then
-  exit 0
-fi
-
-CHANGED_FILES=$(git diff --name-only "$BEFORE" "$AFTER")
-SCRIPTS_CHANGED=$(echo "$CHANGED_FILES" | grep '^bin/' || true)
+SYNCED=""
 
-if [ -z "$SCRIPTS_CHANGED" ]; then
-  log "PULL $BEFORE→$AFTER — no bin/ changes, skipping sync"
-  exit 0
+if [ "$BEFORE" != "$AFTER" ]; then
+  CHANGED_FILES=$(git diff --name-only "$BEFORE" "$AFTER")
+  SCRIPTS_CHANGED=$(echo "$CHANGED_FILES" | grep '^bin/' || true)
+  for script in $SCRIPTS_CHANGED; do
+    filename=$(basename "$script")
+    src="$HIVE_REPO/$script"
+    dst="$INSTALL_DIR/$filename"
+    if [ -f "$src" ] && [ -f "$dst" ]; then
+      cp "$src" "$dst"
+      chmod +x "$dst"
+      SYNCED="$SYNCED $filename"
+    fi
+  done
 fi
 
-SYNCED=""
-for script in $SCRIPTS_CHANGED; do
-  filename=$(basename "$script")
-  src="$HIVE_REPO/$script"
+# Drift check: even if HEAD unchanged, installed files may be stale
+for src in "$HIVE_REPO"/bin/*.sh; do
+  filename=$(basename "$src")
   dst="$INSTALL_DIR/$filename"
-  if [ -f "$src" ] && [ -f "$dst" ]; then
+  [ -f "$dst" ] || continue
+  if ! cmp -s "$src" "$dst"; then
     cp "$src" "$dst"
     chmod +x "$dst"
-    SYNCED="$SYNCED $filename"
+    SYNCED="$SYNCED $filename(drift)"
   fi
 done
 
 if [ -n "$SYNCED" ]; then
-  log "DEPLOY $BEFORE→$AFTER — synced:$SYNCED"
+  log "DEPLOY ${BEFORE:0:7}→${AFTER:0:7} — synced:$SYNCED"
 fi
